title: The Adventure of the Second Stain
synopsis: The Prime Minister and the Secretary for European Affairs consult Holmes in the utmost secrecy: a sensitive diplomatic letter has been stolen from a locked despatch-box, and its publication could provoke a war. The spy Eduardo Lucas is murdered the same night, leading Holmes to investigate both crimes simultaneously.
solution: Lady Hilda Trelawney Hope, the Secretary's wife, stole the letter under blackmail by Lucas, who held an incriminating letter from her past; after Lucas was killed by his wife, Lady Hilda secretly recovered the diplomatic letter from his room and hid it under her drawing-room carpet; Holmes confronts her and replaces the letter before her husband discovers the theft.
notable_elements: One of the Canon's great international espionage cases involving a threat of war
Watson notes this was originally considered too sensitive to publish
The second stain on the carpet shows where the letter was hidden, giving the case its title
Holmes protects Lady Hilda's secret to preserve her marriage, a quietly compassionate act
Intended by Watson as a fitting final adventure for the Return collection
